title: DataTools Streamlit Explorer: Initial App Setup and Deployment
story: As a data analyst using Snowflake, I want a DataTools Streamlit Explorer app deployed in my Snowflake environment so that I can visually explore and query my Snowflake data without writing SQL.
description:
Set up the DataTools Streamlit Explorer project from scratch and deploy it to both Streamlit Community Cloud and the Snowflake native environment.

Setup steps:
1. Create a GitHub repository (DataTools_Streamlit_Explorer) with a development branch
2. Build a basic Streamlit app with Python 3.10+
3. Deploy to Streamlit Community Cloud for staging/testing
4. Deploy to the target Snowflake instance for production use

Snowflake deployment requires:
- GitHub integration in Snowflake (secret, API integration, Git repository objects)
- Manual git fetch in Snowflake when code updates are pushed (Snowflake does not auto-pull unlike Streamlit Cloud)

The app serves as the foundation for subsequent features: data preview, filtering, ordering, GROUP BY, and CSV export.

Staging URL: https://datatoolsprosnow.streamlit.app/
Snowflake deployment: deployed to the Snowflake instance at zckuxij-hl66577.snowflakecomputing.com
acceptance_criteria: GitHub repository DataTools_Streamlit_Explorer is created with a development branch
Basic Streamlit app runs locally with Python 3.10+
App is deployed to Streamlit Community Cloud
App is deployed to the target Snowflake instance
GitHub integration is configured in Snowflake for repository syncing
Filters functionality is implemented for initial demo
ux_changes: New Streamlit-based data explorer application with filter controls for querying Snowflake data. Visual SQL builder interface accessible via web browser.
middleware_changes: Python Streamlit application with Snowflake connectivity, filter logic, and data retrieval. Session state management for interactive widget behavior.
api_changes: Snowflake REST API and SQL API calls for database metadata and query execution within the Streamlit app.
implementation_notes: Streamlit reruns the entire Python script on every widget interaction (not a client-side SPA like React). This causes a 1-2 second delay per interaction which is inherent to the Streamlit architecture. Caching and session state can mitigate this. Snowflake deployment requires manual git fetch (ALTER GIT REPOSITORY ... FETCH) after code pushes since it doesn't auto-sync like Streamlit Community Cloud.
comment_summary: Developer set up the project, deployed to both Streamlit Cloud and Snowflake. Key architectural insight: Streamlit reruns the full script on every interaction, causing inherent latency. Snowflake deployment requires GitHub integration with secrets, API integration, and Git repository objects. Manual repository fetch is needed in Snowflake after code updates. A Loom demo was recorded for review.
